title: Add account_name to AgentResult and invocation threading
analysis:
AgentResult is a dataclass in shared/src/shared/cli_invoke.py with fields: success, output, cost_usd, duration_ms, turns, session_id, structured_output, subtype, stderr. It needs a new 'account_name: str = ""' field to track which Max account executed the invocation.

Two invocation paths need threading:
1. invoke_with_cap_retry (shared/src/shared/cli_invoke.py, line 87): Calls usage_gate.before_invoke() for OAuth token, then invoke_claude_agent(). On cap hit, loops. The active_account_name is already accessed on line 109 for logging — we need to also capture it before invoking and stamp it on the returned result.
2. _invoke_with_session (orchestrator/src/orchestrator/steward.py, line 297): Similar loop with usage_gate.before_invoke() + invoke_agent(). On cap hit, resets session and rebuilds prompt. Same pattern: capture active_account_name before invoke, stamp on result.

UsageGate.active_account_name (line 384 of usage_gate.py) returns str|None — the name of the first non-capped account, or None if all capped. The field default is '' so we coalesce None to ''.

Key insight: capture account_name INSIDE the while loop before each invocation attempt, so after cap-hit + retry the result carries the account that actually served the successful invocation.

Existing tests use MagicMock for the usage gate with active_account_name as a simple attribute. The shared tests are in test_cli_invoke.py (3 tests), steward tests in test_steward.py (~7 test classes). Both use _make_result helpers that construct AgentResult — these may need updating if tests assert on exact fields.

Note: orchestrator/src/orchestrator/agents/invoke.py has its own invoke_with_cap_retry that re-exports AgentResult from shared. That function is NOT in scope per the task description, but it will automatically gain the new field since AgentResult is shared.

steps.description: Write failing test in shared/tests/test_cli_invoke.py: test_account_name_field_defaults_empty — assert AgentResult(success=True, output='ok').account_name == ''. This verifies the new field exists with correct default.
Add 'account_name: str = ""' field to AgentResult dataclass in shared/src/shared/cli_invoke.py (after stderr field, line ~39). This makes step-1 pass.

Write failing test in shared/tests/test_cli_invoke.py: test_account_name_set_from_usage_gate — mock usage_gate with active_account_name='acct-a', invoke_with_cap_retry with no cap hit, assert returned result.account_name == 'acct-a'. Also test_account_name_none_coerced_to_empty — when active_account_name is None, result.account_name == ''.

In invoke_with_cap_retry (shared/src/shared/cli_invoke.py), after the while loop's usage_gate.before_invoke() call and before invoke_claude_agent(), capture account_name = usage_gate.active_account_name if usage_gate else ''. After the break (successful invocation), set result.account_name = account_name or '' before returning.

Write failing test in shared/tests/test_cli_invoke.py: test_account_name_reflects_failover_account — simulate cap hit on first attempt (active_account_name side_effect=['acct-a', 'acct-b']), assert result.account_name == 'acct-b' (the account that served the successful invocation).

Verify step-5 passes with the implementation from step-4 (account_name is captured inside the loop, so it naturally picks up the failover account). If not, adjust capture point to be inside the loop before each invoke call. No code change expected if step-4 was done correctly.

Write failing test in orchestrator/tests/test_steward.py: test_account_name_set_on_result — in TestStewardSessionPersistence or new TestStewardAccountName class, mock usage_gate with active_account_name='max-d', invoke _handle_escalation, capture the result via mock side effects or by inspecting the steward's state/metrics. Assert account_name is 'max-d' on the AgentResult returned by _invoke_with_session.

In _invoke_with_session (orchestrator/src/orchestrator/steward.py), after usage_gate.before_invoke() inside the while loop, capture account_name = self.usage_gate.active_account_name if self.usage_gate else ''. After the cap-hit check succeeds (before return), set result.account_name = account_name or ''.

Write failing test in orchestrator/tests/test_steward.py: test_account_name_reflects_failover_on_cap_hit — simulate cap hit + retry in _invoke_with_session, mock active_account_name as PropertyMock with side_effect=['max-d', 'max-c'], assert the returned result.account_name is 'max-c' (the account used for the successful retry).
Verify step-9 passes with step-8's implementation. Account name is captured inside the loop so failover is naturally reflected. Adjust if needed.

Write test for the no-usage-gate path: test_account_name_empty_without_gate — call invoke_with_cap_retry with usage_gate=None, assert result.account_name == ''. This confirms the coalescing works when there's no gate.
Verify step-11 passes (the 'if usage_gate else ""' guard handles this). Run full test suite for both shared/tests/test_cli_invoke.py and orchestrator/tests/test_steward.py to confirm all tests pass.

design_decisions.decision: Place account_name field last in AgentResult (after stderr)
design_decisions.rationale: Follows the pattern of optional metadata fields being at the end with defaults. Avoids breaking any positional construction (all existing callers use keyword args or the _make_result helpers).

design_decisions.decision: Default to empty string '' rather than None
design_decisions.rationale: Consistent with session_id, subtype, and stderr fields which all default to ''. Empty string is falsy and avoids None-checks downstream. Coalesce None from active_account_name with 'or ""'.

design_decisions.decision: Capture account_name inside the while loop (before each invocation attempt)
design_decisions.rationale: On cap-hit + retry, the active account changes via failover. Capturing inside the loop ensures the result carries the name of the account that actually served the successful invocation, not the one that was capped.

Task scope explicitly names only the shared invoke_with_cap_retry and steward's _invoke_with_session. The orchestrator's invoke_with_cap_retry inherits the new AgentResult field automatically (it re-exports from shared). Threading account_name there can be a follow-up.

design_decisions.decision: Set account_name on the result object after invocation rather than passing it into invoke_claude_agent
design_decisions.rationale: account_name is invocation metadata, not an invoke parameter. Mutating the returned dataclass is simpler and doesn't change the invoke_claude_agent signature. Same pattern used for session_id capture in steward.

reuse.how: Extend existing _make_result(**overrides) to support account_name in overrides dict; no changes needed since it uses **defaults.update(overrides) pattern.
Extend existing _make_result(cost, turns, session_id, success) with account_name='' kwarg for tests that need to assert on it.
Follow the established pattern: gate = MagicMock(), gate.active_account_name = 'acct-name'. Already used in existing cap-hit tests.
Read-only access — already returns str|None for the first non-capped account name. No changes needed to UsageGate.
